commit.message:
Build 125: fix cold-launch notification navigation

Move restorePendingNag() before slow awaits in AuthenticatedTabView.task.
onChange(of: pendingNagId) won't fire if the value was already set before
the view appeared — cold-launch drain sets it before SwiftUI renders.
By checking UserDefaults immediately at task start (before family load
and sync awaits), navigation happens the moment AuthenticatedTabView appears.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove duplicate root page causing Vercel manifest error

app/page.tsx and app/(app)/page.tsx both mapped to URL '/'.
Next.js picked the static app/page.tsx as winner, leaving
app/(app)/page.tsx as an unreachable ghost route with no
client-reference-manifest.js — exactly what Vercel errored on.

Deleting app/page.tsx makes app/(app)/page.tsx the sole handler
for '/', properly protected by AuthGuard via the (app) layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: anchor orbits to server once then run at client game_speed

Dead-reckoning the server's game_seconds was wrong — it advances at only
~10 sim-sec/real-sec (speed-independent), so planets appeared frozen at
any game speed. The correct approach: snap _sim_elapsed to server's
game_seconds on first poll to get the right starting position, then let
advance(dt) run at client game_speed locally. No re-syncing after anchor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add SMT Fill signal to QTS (NQ fills gap while ES doesn't)

Adds smt_fill_bull / smt_fill_bear signals: triggered when NQ taps into
a shared gap (NWOG/NDOG) while ES does NOT fill its corresponding portion.
This is the SMT divergence-on-fill pattern, distinct from ssmt_fill
(where both assets fill together).

- New signal types in QTSSignal union
- smtFilled flag on QTSFvg to prevent repeated signals per gap
- Emerald label for bull fills, rose for bear fills on chart
- QTSDashboard: amber=ssmt_fill, emerald=smt_fill_bull, rose=smt_fill_bear

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: run entire PDF rendering pipeline on MainActor to fix blank iOS output

CGPDFDocument, CGPDFPage, and CGContext.drawPDFPage all require main-thread
affinity on iOS. The previous fix moved only drawPDFPage to MainActor while
CGPDFDocument/CGPDFPage were still created on @DocRenderActor and passed
across — still causing blank output.

Introduce renderPagesOnMain(@MainActor) containing the full rendering
pipeline. renderPages(@DocRenderActor) is now a thin wrapper that calls
`try await MainActor.run { try renderPagesOnMain(...) }`. No CoreGraphics
PDF objects ever cross thread boundaries.

Also removes the now-unnecessary Unchecked<T> wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Migrate all state management from JSON files to UsageModel SQLite

Eliminate all JSON state file references (usage_cache.json, synthetic_cache.json,
fallback_state.json, api_backoff.json) from production code. UsageModel is now
the single source of truth for backoff, fallback, API cache, and cost accumulation.

Production changes (6 files):
- api_client: Remove JSON cache/backoff paths, use UsageModel for 429 handling
- hook: Remove fallback_state_path param, use UsageModel for cost accumulation
- pacing_engine: Remove USAGE_CACHE_PATH/SYNTHETIC_CACHE_PATH, use UsageModel
- profile_cache: Remove backoff_path param, use UsageModel.is_in_backoff()
- usage_model: Add get_current_usage(), get_api_cache(), enhanced fallback methods
- user_commands: Remove JSON state path params from status command

Test changes (8 files):
- Rewrite all tests to use UsageModel(db_path=) for isolated SQLite state
- Fix patch targets for local imports (pacemaker.usage_model.UsageModel)
- Replace JSON file setup helpers with UsageModel method calls
- Net reduction of 338 lines across 14 files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

commit.message:
feat: phase 8 — Observable dashboards, parquet backup, live chart preview

## Dashboard system (new)
- Agent `create_dashboard` tool generates Observable Framework .md files
- `src/dashboards/` module: CRUD REST endpoints (list/get/put/delete) + shared config
- `jonas.config.js` written once per tenant — API URL never appears in .md files
- `DashboardsPage.tsx`: sidebar list, edit/preview toggle, live Observable Plot rendering
- Preview executes `js` blocks in-browser: data fetched from API, charts rendered via Plot/Inputs
- JS block classifier prevents setup/declaration blocks from being eval'd as expressions

## Parquet backup storage (new)
- `src/storage/parquet.py`: tenant-scoped layout — bronze (append), silver/gold (latest.parquet)
- Called after every ingest (webhook/CSV/JSON) and transform execution
- Fail-safe: logs warnings, never raises, primary DuckDB unaffected

## UI improvements
- Renamed home nav "Dashboard" → "Overview" to avoid collision with Dashboards page
- Lineage: arrowhead markers only attached when edge is visible (was showing on dimmed edges)
- DataChart: fixed useMemo-first pattern so chart type resolved before ref mounts
- DataChart: fixed filter(Boolean) stripping numeric zero values
- Agent SSE: surface real LLM errors (429/401/503) instead of generic "Internal server error"
- Agent prompt: SQL results formatted as markdown tables; dashboard guidance added

## Security + config
- Rate limiting via SlowAPI (`src/limiter.py`)
- Connector config encryption via Fernet (`src/security/crypto.py`)
- `dashboards_root` config setting; Docker volume mount for data/dashboards/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(usage): cache error responses from usage API

Error responses (e.g. rate_limit_error) were being written to cache
and then immediately deleted by ParseBody, causing every claudeline
instance to hit the API on every invocation. Keep error responses
cached for the standard TTL so all instances share the result.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 5a noise models — depolarizing + SPAM + named device profiles

circuit.run({ noise: 'aria-1' | NoiseParams }) — opt-in stochastic noise.

Depolarizing channel:
  dep1(sv, q, p1, rand)  — random Pauli X/Y/Z after each single-qubit gate
  dep2(sv, a, b, p2, rand) — random non-identity 2-qubit Pauli (15 channels)
    after each 2-qubit gate (cnot, controlled, swap, two)

SPAM: pMeas flips each mid-circuit measured bit and each final readout bit
independently, modelling readout errors without perturbing the quantum state.

Named device profiles (DEVICE_NOISE):
  'aria-1'  — p1=0.0003, p2=0.005,  pMeas=0.004
  'forte-1' — p1=0.0001, p2=0.002,  pMeas=0.002
  'harmony' — p1=0.001,  p2=0.015,  pMeas=0.01

Fast path preserved: pure circuits with no noise simulate once + sample N.
Noise forces the per-shot path (stochastic — must re-simulate every shot).

Exports: NoiseParams added to public API.
16 new tests covering 1Q/2Q depolarizing, SPAM, named profiles, custom
params. 338 tests total, ~150ms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Auto-generate CAP cache in Maven build under target/

The CAP cache is now auto-generated during the native Maven build
(at the 'package' phase) when absent, via the new exec-maven-plugin
execution that calls generate-cap-cache.sh with --skip-jar-build.
The cache lives in target/cap-cache/ instead of being committed to git,
and is regenerated on 'mvn clean'.

The generate-cap-cache.sh script has been enhanced with early-exit logic
and argument parsing (--skip-jar-build, --cap-cache-dir=PATH) to support
both manual and Maven-driven execution. pom.xml now runs the CAP cache
generation at the package phase, before native-maven-plugin.

commit.message:
Fix protocol elements pulled from wrong competition + case-insensitive badge lookup

getElements() had no competition scope, so result IDs that collide
across competitions (e.g. both starting from 1) returned elements from
other events — causing a different execution value (Landed → Clean) to
appear for the same element code. Fixed by:

1. Adding optional competitionId param to getElements() and filtering
   on it when provided.
2. Restructuring protocol.js to fetch the result first, then pass
   result.competition_id when fetching elements in the parallel step.
3. Making the exec badge class lookup case-insensitive so entries like
   'QUarter' correctly resolve to exec-Quarter instead of exec-default.

fix(poe2/tests): real-time PTY logging via observer callback

run_agent_capturing gains on_line: Option<Box<dyn Fn(String)+Send+'static>>.
Called from reader thread for every PTY line as it arrives — callers get
live visibility without waiting for the run to complete.

Test wires this to HarnessLog (now Arc<Mutex<File>>, cloneable). Each PTY
line is logged immediately with its index and parsed event type if
recognised. PARSE FAIL lines are flagged. done_rx timeout reduced to 60s.
reader_thread.join() given 5s timeout to prevent hang if slave fd leaks.
